ucs.title: [LLM] Volatile Cedar 'Caterpillar' web shell drop via IIS/Apache exploitation
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's signature chain: internet-facing web server gets exploited (T1190) after recon scanning, then the worker process (w3wp.exe / httpd / nginx) writes an ASPX/PHP/JSP file into the web root and immediately spawns a shell — the canonical Caterpillar/Explosive-RAT staging pattern Volatile Cedar has used since 2012 against universities, telcos, and defense contractors per Check Point and ClearSky reporting.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_name) as files values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","httpd","nginx.exe","nginx","tomcat*.exe","java.exe") (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asp" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.php" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.jsp") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*/htdocs/*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*/webapps/*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\wwwroot\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","java.exe","tomcat*.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","net.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","sh","bash") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where lastTime - firstTime < 1800 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.rationale: Volatile Cedar's intrusion playbook (per Check Point 2015 and ClearSky 2021) is uniquely web-server-centric: they perform automated vuln/wordlist scanning (T1595.002/.003), exploit unpatched IIS/Tomcat/PHP apps (T1190), and drop the Caterpillar web shell (T1505.003) which is then used to run reconnaissance commands and stage Explosive RAT (T1105). ucs.title: [LLM] Volatile Cedar Explosive RAT staging — web-shell-driven tool transfer to compromised server
ucs.description: Catches the second stage of Volatile Cedar's chain: after Caterpillar gives them an interactive shell on the IIS/Apache host, they use the same web-server worker process as the parent of certutil/bitsadmin/powershell-WebClient/curl/wget to pull Explosive RAT (T1105) from attacker infrastructure, often hosted on look-alike Lebanese or academic domains.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","java.exe","tomcat*.exe") AND (Processes.process_name IN ("certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*urlcache*" OR Processes.process="*-urlcache*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadFile*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*WebClient*" OR Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*" OR Processes.process="*/transfer *")) by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" AND (All_Traffic.app="http" OR All_Traffic.app="https" OR All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080,8443)) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port | rename All_Traffic.src as dest | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let webProcs = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe"]);
let livingOff = dynamic(["certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (webProcs)
| where FileName in~ (livingOff)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-urlcache","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","WebClient","/transfer ","-OutFile")
| extend hostFromCmd = extract(@"https?://([^/\s\"']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where RemotePort in (80,443,8080,8443)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (livingOff)
    | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, NetInitiator=InitiatingProcessId
  ) on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (Timestamp - 2m .. Timestamp + 5m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProc=FileName, ProcessCommandLine, hostFromCmd, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Pivoting from web shell to Explosive RAT is Volatile Cedar's documented MO (Check Point Lebanese Cedar report; ClearSky 2021 Lebanese Cedar APT). Crucially, the parent process is w3wp/httpd — not a user shell — which is virtually never legitimate: web server workers should serve HTTP, not invoke certutil/bitsadmin/Invoke-WebRequest with outbound URLs.
